title: AI Inside Your Existing Tools
summary: Get more value from AI where your work already lives by matching embedded features to specific jobs and review standards.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Why Embedded AI Often Wins
lessons.subtitle: Context already in the workspace can matter more than raw model novelty.
lessons.objective: Identify when embedded AI features inside your current tools are the best option.
lessons.useWhen: Context already in the workspace can matter more than raw model novelty.
lessons.whyItMatters: The strongest AI workflow is often the one with the least friction and the most relevant context already attached.

coreConcepts.title: Context Is The Advantage
coreConcepts.text: Email, docs, tickets, chats, and calendars already contain the material that general assistants often need you to paste manually.

coreConcepts.title: Workflow Fit Matters
coreConcepts.text: A slightly weaker model inside the right tool can still produce better outcomes because setup friction is lower.

coreConcepts.title: Review Standards Stay The Same
coreConcepts.text: Better integration does not remove the need to verify high-risk output.

workedExample.scenario: A manager uses a standalone tool for meeting follow-ups even though the notes already live in their workspace.
workedExample.approach: They compare the embedded AI feature against the external workflow on the same meeting.
workedExample.result: The embedded tool wins because setup is lighter and the context is closer.
workedExample.verification: They still check owners, dates, and factual accuracy before sending.

artifact.label: Decision Rule
artifact.title: Use embedded AI when
items: The source material already lives there

doThisNow.task: List one task that would be easier inside your current workspace than in a separate AI tool.
steps: State how you will review the output.

commonMistakes: Ignoring embedded tools you already pay for.
Assuming embedded AI means safer output automatically.
Switching tools without comparing the workflow end to end.

roleVariants.manager: Model safe adoption through small pilots, clear examples, and lightweight team habits.
roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Evaluate Embedded Features By Task
lessons.subtitle: Judge the feature on what you actually do, not on screenshots or launch claims.
lessons.objective: Test embedded AI features against real workflow tasks.
lessons.useWhen: Judge the feature on what you actually do, not on screenshots or launch claims.
lessons.whyItMatters: Embedded AI varies widely. The right question is whether it improves your work inside the tool, not whether it looks impressive in a demo.

coreConcepts.title: Use Real Inputs
coreConcepts.text: Test on the emails, docs, chats, or tickets you actually work with.

coreConcepts.title: Measure Time To Final
coreConcepts.text: The best feature usually produces the shortest path to a reviewed final output.

coreConcepts.title: Check Integration Quality
coreConcepts.text: See whether the feature actually respects the context and structure of the tool.

workedExample.scenario: A writer evaluates an embedded document assistant.
workedExample.approach: They test outline generation, editing, and recap drafting on real documents.
workedExample.result: They discover the feature is strong for editing but weak for initial synthesis.
workedExample.verification: They compare the same task against a general assistant to understand the tradeoff.

doThisNow.task: Run one real task through an embedded AI feature and score the result.

commonMistakes: Assuming a native integration means the output is automatically correct.

roleVariants.manager: Model safe adoption through small pilots, clear examples, and lightweight team habits.
roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Use Workspace Context Better
lessons.subtitle: The win comes from asking with the right nearby material, not from the tool label.
lessons.objective: Write better prompts for AI features embedded in docs, chat, and productivity suites.
lessons.useWhen: The win comes from asking with the right nearby material, not from the tool label.
lessons.whyItMatters: Even inside your workspace, AI still needs clear intent, constraints, and review instructions.

coreConcepts.title: Point To The Right Material
coreConcepts.text: Call out which email thread, notes, paragraph, or document section should drive the answer.

coreConcepts.title: State The Action
coreConcepts.text: Ask for the exact outcome: summary, reply, next-step list, rewrite, or agenda.

coreConcepts.title: Limit The Output
coreConcepts.text: Embedded AI works better when the output is bounded and immediately usable.

workedExample.scenario: An operator asks an embedded email assistant for a reply and gets a generic response.
workedExample.approach: They reference the exact thread, the approved facts, and the tone needed.
workedExample.result: The draft becomes more useful and less generic.
workedExample.verification: They check that the reply reflects the current thread and does not invent commitments.

doThisNow.task: Rewrite one embedded AI request to point to exact source material and a bounded action.

commonMistakes: Prompting vaguely just because the tool is nearby.
Letting the model choose the source context.
Skipping the same review rules you would use elsewhere.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Build Stable Integration Patterns
lessons.subtitle: The best embedded AI uses become habits, not experiments.
lessons.objective: Turn successful embedded AI use cases into reliable daily or weekly routines.
lessons.useWhen: The best embedded AI uses become habits, not experiments.
lessons.whyItMatters: A tool feature matters only when it changes how work gets done consistently.

coreConcepts.title: Capture The Trigger
coreConcepts.text: Know what event starts the workflow: weekly review, incoming request, meeting end, or document draft.

coreConcepts.title: Capture The Prompt Pattern
coreConcepts.text: Save the instructions and source references that work repeatedly.

coreConcepts.title: Capture The Final Review
coreConcepts.text: Every integration pattern should include the human checkpoint.

workedExample.scenario: A manager uses an embedded chat feature inconsistently.
workedExample.approach: They standardize one pattern for meeting prep and one pattern for follow-up communication.
workedExample.result: The feature becomes part of real work instead of a novelty button.
workedExample.verification: They review whether the pattern still holds after a few uses or needs more context.

doThisNow.task: Document one embedded AI pattern you want to use repeatedly.

commonMistakes: Relying on memory instead of storing patterns.
Using the feature ad hoc with no repeatable structure.

roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Design Your Daily AI Workflow
lessons.subtitle: Mix embedded and general tools intentionally instead of randomly.
lessons.objective: Create a daily workflow that combines your core AI tools with less switching and more consistency.
lessons.useWhen: Mix embedded and general tools intentionally instead of randomly.
lessons.whyItMatters: A coherent workflow reduces tool sprawl and helps you know where each task belongs.

coreConcepts.title: Use The Closest Useful Tool
coreConcepts.text: If the work lives in your workspace, start there. Switch tools only when the task genuinely demands it.

coreConcepts.title: Separate Creation From Verification
coreConcepts.text: Embedded AI may draft inside the workspace while source-forward tools or manual review handle verification.

coreConcepts.title: Keep The Stack Small
coreConcepts.text: A small set of reliable patterns beats a scattered set of partially adopted features.

workedExample.scenario: A business IC jumps between five AI tools during a normal day.
workedExample.approach: They map which tasks belong in the workspace, which need a general assistant, and which need source-first research.
workedExample.result: The day becomes less fragmented and the outputs become easier to trust.
workedExample.verification: They review whether the new workflow reduces switching and cleanup rather than adding more tools to manage.

doThisNow.task: Map your current AI tasks into a smaller daily workflow.

commonMistakes: Using different tools for the same job with no reason.
Treating embedded AI and general AI as competitors instead of complements.
Growing the stack without redesigning the workflow.

outcomes: Choose when embedded AI beats a separate tool.
nextModuleBridge: Next, package the workflows that work into reusable playbooks for yourself and your team.
